Got it! Black Knight Butterfly Bush is a good one.  Once established   usually after the first full season   it's pretty drought-tolerant. In Farmington summers you'd probably water deeply once or twice a week during hot, dry stretches, less when you get rain. The first year is the critical one: keep it consistently moist while it's getting roots down, so maybe every 2 3 days if it's dry and hot.  Where were you thinking of putting it?

up the weather from the past week.
